SEVERE FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED IN THE CITY OF WHITE SPRINGS WITH ROADS WASHED OUT TO A DEPTH OF 8 TO 12 FEET. THE FIRST ROADWAY WASHED OUT WAS 1ST STREET WHICH WAS COMPLETELY SEVERED. THE WATER THEN FLOWED ALONG A STREAM BED TO JEWETT STREET WHICH WAS ALSO COMPLETELY SEVERED TO A DEPTH OF 12 FEET. ONE CULVET PIPE WAS COMPLETELY WASHED AWAY AND HAS NOT BE LOCATED. KENDRICK STREET WAS THEN FLOODED AND APPROXIMATELY 20 FEET OF ROAD BED WASHED AWAY OR UNDERMINED.
